# DESIGN-LOCK.md schema reference

The DESIGN-LOCK.md file is the structured contract between cv-sketch and cv-create. cv-sketch writes it from the mockup; cv-create reads it to write Splunk Canvas code. Format is YAML for human readability and easy diffs.

## Full schema

```yaml
# DESIGN-LOCK.md — auto-generated by cv-sketch, consumed by cv-create
# Do not edit by hand unless iterating with cv-create.

project:
  app_id: <string>                    # matches scope context, e.g. "redbull_racing_viz"
  display_name: <string>              # e.g. "Red Bull Racing F1"
  version: <semver>                   # e.g. "1.0.0"
  format: <classic | extension | both>

# === GLOBAL DESIGN TOKENS ===
global:
  brand:
    primary:   "<hex>"
    secondary: "<hex>"
    accent:    "<hex>"

  palette_dark:
    bg:          "<hex>"
    panel:       "<hex>"
    panel_hi:    "<hex>"
    edge:        "<rgba|hex>"
    text:        "<hex>"
    text_dim:    "<hex>"
    text_faint:  "<hex>"
    series:      ["<hex>","<hex>","<hex>","<hex>","<hex>"]

  palette_light:
    # NOT an inversion of dark. Independently designed.
    bg:          "<hex>"
    panel:       "<hex>"
    panel_hi:    "<hex>"
    edge:        "<rgba|hex>"
    text:        "<hex>"
    text_dim:    "<hex>"
    text_faint:  "<hex>"
    series:      ["<hex>","<hex>","<hex>","<hex>","<hex>"]

  typography:
    display:
      family:        "<google-fonts name>"
      fallback:      "<comma-separated fallback chain>"
      embed:         base64
      weights_used:  [<int>, <int>, ...]
    mono:
      family:        "<google-fonts name>"
      fallback:      "<comma-separated fallback chain>"
      embed:         base64
      weights_used:  [<int>, <int>, ...]

  scale_spacing: [4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32, 48, 64]   # 4pt grid
  scale_radius:  [0, 2, 4, 8, 12]

  # Stage A commitments persisted for cv-create's sub-decisions
  commitments:
    hero_archetype:     <string>
    domain_unique:      "<one sentence>"
    anti_references:
      - "<concrete thing 1>"
      - "<concrete thing 2>"
      - "<concrete thing 3>"

# === DASHBOARD LAYOUT ===
dashboard:
  canvas:
    width: 1920                       # always 1920 minimum
    height: 1080                      # always 1080 minimum

  background:
    type: <gradient_png | flat | radial>
    gradient_spec:                    # only if type == gradient_png
      from: "<hex>"
      to:   "<hex>"
      direction: <radial_top_center | vertical | diagonal>

  panels:
    - viz: <viz_name>
      position: { x: <int>, y: <int>, w: <int>, h: <int> }
    # ... one entry per panel

  tabs:                               # optional, only if pack has 7+ vizs
    - label: "<UPPERCASE LABEL>"
      panels: [<viz_name>, <viz_name>, ...]

# === PER-VIZ SPECS ===
vizs:
  - name: <viz_name>                  # lowercase_with_underscores
    type: <gauge | kpi | bars | grid | line | timeline | radar | progress | scatter | network | custom>
    data_contract:
      required:
        - { field: <field_name>, type: <string|number>, example: "<example value>" }
      optional:
        - { field: <field_name>, type: <string|number>, example: "<example value>" }
    visual_spec:
      shape_language: "<one phrase, e.g. 'capacitor' or 'instrument-panel-readout'>"
      geometry:
        # specific to viz type. Numeric values, not vague terms.
        # examples:
        aspect_ratio:        <string, e.g. "1:3.2">
        segment_count:       <int>
        segment_gap:         "<int>px"
        hero_text_ratio:     <float, 0-1>
        # ... etc
      fills:
        # one entry per fillable element
        background:
          type: <solid | linear_gradient | radial_gradient | solid_with_carbon_overlay>
          base: "<hex>"
          # if gradient:
          from: "<hex>"
          to:   "<hex>"
          direction: <vertical_down | radial_top | etc>
          # if overlay:
          overlay_pattern: <carbon_twill_2x2 | dot_matrix | noise>
          overlay_opacity: <float>
        background_light:           # light theme override — often simpler
          type: solid
          base: "<hex>"
          overlay: none             # skip effects that don't work on light
      typography:
        # per-element type spec
        hero:    { family: <display|mono>, weight: <int>, transform: <uppercase|none>, color: <text_token> }
        sub:     { family: <display|mono>, weight: <int>, transform: <uppercase|none>, color: <text_token> }
        # ... etc per element
      effects:
        # named effects with concrete numeric values
        breathe_animation:
          property: <drop_shadow | box_shadow | opacity>
          from: "<starting value>"
          to:   "<ending value>"
          duration_ms: <int>
          easing: <ease_in_out | linear | etc>
          apply_on: "<condition, e.g. 'charge > 80'>"
        # ... etc
      hover:
        target: <segment | bar | row | none>
        effect: <brighten_1.3x | tint_accent | scale_1.05 | etc>
    visual_reference_html: |
      <!-- The EXACT HTML+CSS block from mockup.html for this viz -->
      <!-- Includes both [data-theme="dark"] and [data-theme="light"] -->
      <!-- This is the visual contract cv-create translates to Canvas -->
      <div class="...">...</div>
      <style>
        ...
      </style>

# === DASHBOARD-LEVEL INTERACTIONS ===
interactions:
  drilldowns:
    - from: <viz_name>
      on: <click | row_click | segment_click>
      sets_token: <token_name>
      to_search: "<SPL with $token$ refs>"

  inputs:
    - type: <time_range | dropdown | text>
      token: <token_name>
      default: "<default value>"
```

## Validation rules

When cv-sketch writes DESIGN-LOCK.md, it must satisfy:

- `project.format` is exactly one of `classic`, `extension`, `both`
- `global.palette_dark` and `global.palette_light` both exist, both have all required keys
- `global.typography.display.family` and `mono.family` are NOT on the reflex-reject list (cross-check against [typography.md](typography.md))
- Every panel in `dashboard.panels[]` references a viz that exists in `vizs[]`
- Every viz has both `visual_spec` and `visual_reference_html`
- `visual_spec.geometry` uses numeric values, not vague terms (no `"large"`, no `"medium"`)
- Every viz has `fills.background_light` explicitly set (even if `overlay: none`)

## Why YAML and not JSON

- Multi-line strings are first-class — `visual_reference_html` can contain raw HTML+CSS without escaping
- Comments are first-class — cv-create can explain its delta interpretations inline
- Diffs are readable — `git diff DESIGN-LOCK.md` shows exactly what an iteration changed

## How cv-create consumes this

cv-create reads DESIGN-LOCK.md once and:

1. Generates `shared/theme.js` from `global.palette_dark/light` and `global.typography`
2. For each viz in `vizs[]`:
   - Reads `data_contract` → wires `formatData`
   - Reads `visual_spec` → generates formatter.html controls
   - Reads `visual_reference_html` → translates CSS to Canvas in `_renderDark` / `_renderLight`
   - Re-reads `global.commitments.anti_references` at the start of each viz to avoid regression
3. Generates dashboard.xml from `dashboard.panels` and `interactions`

See cv-create's references for the exact translation rules.
